title: ranunculus lappaceus var. pimpinellifolius
intro: Ranunculus lappaceus var. pimpinellifolius, also known as Creeping Buttercup, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Ranunculus genus. It is native to the western United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The Creeping Buttercup plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
